propositions.content: The fact that AI researchers are routinely surprised by their own models' emergent behaviors demonstrates that LLM output cannot be made reliably predictable.
propositions.note: Core skeptical challenge to AI reliability

propositions.content: RITAM's goal should be AI governance (bounded, observable, repairable behavior) rather than AI reliability (predictable output) — conflating these two goals is a category error that sets an unreachable success condition.
propositions.note: Governance vs reliability distinction — scope refinement for P4b

propositions.content: The Kill Test conflict detection operates on content hashes within singular categories (structural detection), not on semantic meaning — it cannot detect two propositions that express the same idea in different words.
propositions.note: Audit A1 — structural not semantic detection

propositions.content: As of v0.1, 6 of the 9 substrate primitives (State, Memory, Ontology, Coordination, Temporal, Repair) are theoretically specified but unimplemented and unvalidated by running code.
propositions.note: Audit A3 — honesty about implementation scope

results.note: Core skeptical challenge to AI reliability
Governance vs reliability distinction — scope refinement for P4b
Audit A1 — structural not semantic detection
Audit A3 — honesty about implementation scope
